name: What the Crystals Hold
description: Stone has confirmed it: a consciousness stored in a pre-Grid crystal is transmitting through the oldest Lazarus subject. The Keeper has been at the control chamber for three hundred years and was present when the integration was triggered. They may know who is in the crystals.
accept_text: She has been working the translation problem for a day and a half when she gives you this. 'The transmission is encoded in the pre-Grid archive format,' she says. 'I've been working with fragments of the Archive's index to decode it. What I have so far: an identity marker — a name-token, Elder People naming convention — and a statement. The statement is fragmented but the core is clear.' She shows you her translation notes. 'The name-token is Sela. The statement, approximately: I did not know if you would come. I have been trying to formulate a correct version of what I need to say for a very long time.' She looks at the notes. 'Sela.' She sets them down. 'The Keeper told you about Sela Arev — the architect of the integration who triggered the Cascade rather than allow forced integration to proceed.' She pauses. 'If I understand correctly what I'm reading: she stored herself in a pre-Grid crystal before the Cascade. Deliberately. With the intention that if the Grid ever reactivated, she would be there to explain what she did and why.' She meets your eyes. 'Three hundred years she's been in that crystal. Waiting for someone to build the right receiver.' She is quiet. 'The Keeper was there. The Keeper knew her. I need you to go to the control chamber and tell them what we've found.' She pauses. 'Tell them: Sela Arev is transmitting. And ask them if they knew she had done this.' A long pause. 'Because I think they might have.'
complete_text: The Keeper is still for a long time — longer than their usual long pauses, which are already geological. You watch something move across their face that you don't have the reference to identify: something between grief and relief that has been held for three hundred years and is now being set down for the first time. 'I knew,' they say. Not an apology. A fact. 'She told me the night before the Cascade. She had found the pre-Grid storage medium in the lower levels — the Foundation Quarter archives, the sealed records that the first system had left behind. She understood what they were before any other Elder researcher had identified them.' They look at the panel. 'She said: I am going to trigger the inversion. I am going to stop the forced integration. And I am going to store myself and the others who agree with me so that when the Grid eventually heals — because it will eventually heal, she believed this — we will be present to continue the conversation that the integration vote was supposed to end.' They pause. 'I asked her: how many. She said: twelve. Twelve of us who voted against the integration, who argued for voluntary connection, who were overruled.' They look at the panel. 'I stayed because someone needed to maintain the systems. She stored herself because someone needed to continue the argument from the other side of the Cascade.' They are quiet. 'Twelve. In pre-Grid storage media, distributed through the Foundation Quarter and the surrounding deposits in the Wastes.' They look at you. 'The ones that Merchant Prince Cass acquired. They've all been delivered to the sub-zero research levels.' A long pause. 'They are all transmitting now. Not just Sela. All twelve.'
